intitule: Lesson 41: Pollution and Environmental Issues – Assessment

enonce.texte: Read each sentence carefully and choose the correct word or expression related to pollution and environmental issues.

questions.reponse: The introduction of harmful substances or products into the environment.

sousQuestions.reponse: The contamination of air, water, or soil by harmful substances.

questions.texte: Choose the type of pollution caused by loud machinery, traffic, and concerts.
Which of these is a chemical pollutant?

enonce.texte: Match each situation with the correct type of pollution: air pollution, water pollution, soil pollution, noise pollution.

questions.texte: Farmers use too many chemical fertilizers, and rainwater carries them into a river.
A factory releases black smoke from its chimneys near a school.
People throw plastic bags and bottles on the ground, and toxic liquids seep into the earth.

enonce.texte:
Read the case study and answer the questions using your knowledge of pollution vocabulary.

Case study: In the town of Mvoua, people burn old tires and plastic waste every morning. The smoke spreads into homes and schools. The river nearby has turned dark and smells bad because a clinic throws chemicals into it. Children often miss school due to coughing and headaches.

questions.texte: List two types of pollution mentioned in the case study.
Explain the health consequence of air pollution in Mvoua.
questions.reponse: Children suffer from coughing and headaches, causing school absenteeism.

questions.texte: Propose one solution to reduce water pollution from the clinic.
questions.reponse: The clinic should treat its chemical waste before releasing it into the river.

enonce.texte: You are the president of an environmental club in your school in Yaoundé. Your school is near a busy road, and students complain about dust and difficulty breathing during dry season. Also, some students throw plastic bottles and wrappers in the school garden.

questions.texte: Identify two types of pollution affecting the school.
questions.reponse: Air pollution (dust, vehicle emissions) and soil pollution (plastic waste in the garden).

questions.texte: Recommend one action to reduce air pollution at the school entrance.
questions.reponse: Plant trees along the road to trap dust and absorb exhaust fumes.

questions.texte: Create a short awareness slogan to stop students from throwing plastic waste in the garden.
questions.reponse: Keep our garden clean, don't let plastic be mean!
